raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Self-report of completed reparative action qualified by an ethical exception clause",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "identifiable others have been harmed by the speaker",
    "direct amends are feasible in the majority of cases",
    "the speaker possesses reliable judgment to determine when amends would cause injury",
    "non-injury to self or others is a higher-order constraint than making amends"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Coherent; the exception clause prevents the rule from becoming self-defeating or harmful",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "Speaker has actually caused harm, can accurately forecast injury, and operates in a therapeutic context where behavioral follow-through is being tracked",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "No genuine harms exist, the exception is invoked to avoid accountability, or 'injury' is defined so broadly that amends are never attempted"
}
